Anthropic is withholding Claude Mythos — its most powerful model — over safety concerns. Preview pricing: $2,500/M tokens. The frontier AI you are planning on may not be available when you need it.

---

TWEET 2
This is a new category of business risk: capability gating. A provider decides their model is too powerful to release broadly. Your product roadmap built on that model just hit an unplanned delay.

---

TWEET 3
Regulatory pressure and safety protocols are becoming real constraints on model availability. The most capable AI will not always be the most accessible. Plan accordingly.

---

TWEET 4
Strategic response for CTOs: diversify model dependencies. Build internal evaluation capability. Design architectures that can swap models without rewriting your stack. Resilience over optimization.

---

TWEET 5
Open-source models like Gemma 4 become strategic insurance in this environment. When closed providers gate capability, your fallback determines whether you ship or stall.

## Fact-check notes
- Anthropic is holding back its Claude Mythos model due to safety concerns, despite it being described as their most advanced conversational model with a focus on ethical AI interactions (Source: YouTube AI roundup April 10, 2026; af.net April 2026 model tracker)
- Claude Mythos Preview is listed at $2,500 per million tokens on model tracking platforms, indicating frontier-tier pricing for limited preview access (Source: llm-stats.com, April 2026 model listings)
